compiled_state.title: Fidelity reviewer success_measurable dimension rejects valid observability-based success criteria that cannot be verified within the closing session
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description: The Agent Clarity reviewer's success_measurable dimension scores below 4 when a success criterion describes a validation mechanism that runs post-delivery (e.g., dogfooding sessions, post-delivery audits) rather than within the session that closes the epic. This forces epics to either drop post-delivery validation criteria or contort them into session-verifiable form, which undermines the intent of observability-based success criteria. The reviewer should accept success criteria that ensure the right observability is in place to monitor success, even when the actual measurement happens after the epic closes.

comments.body: Behavioral assertion (Step 5 LLM exemption): After fix, agent-clarity reviewer MUST accept post-delivery validation mechanisms as valid success criteria. Grep for 'post-delivery' or 'dogfooding' in reviewer guidance.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
